MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-06-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, OSC RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - THE COURT ISSUED THE FOLLOWING RULING: 1) THE DEFENDANTS ARE PROHIBITED FROM INITIATING ANY CONTRACT WITH FRONTIER; 2) DEFENDANTS ARE PROHIBITED FROM ANY FURTHER USE OF ANY INFORMATION TAKEN FROM THE PLAINTIFF, REQUIRING DEFENDANTS TO RETURN OR DESTROY ALL COPIES OF ANY SUCH INFORMATION GENERATED FROM IT WITH THE SUPERVISION OF PLAINTIFF; 3) DEFENDANTS MUST ALSO PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFF A LIST OF RECIPIENTS OF E-MAILS; 4) THE DEFENDANTS MUST NOT DESTROY ANY COMMUNICATIONS, WHICH THEY HAVE A RECORD WITH ANY CLIENT OR PROSPECTIVE CLIENTS TO WHOM THOSE E-MAILS WERE SENT WITHOUT THE SUPERVISION OF THE PLAINTIFF; 5) COOPERATE IN TERMS OF COMMUNICATING TO CORRECT RECORD. BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $75,000.00 TO BE POSTED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288

Osc Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER G. BERTRAND & ATTY. JULIAN MACK OF THE BUCHALTER NEMER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 227-0900 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL DERGOSITS & ATTY. IGOR SHOIKET OF THE DERGOSITS & NOAH LLP, TEL. NO. 705-6377 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; OSC Re Preliminary Injunction - THE COURT ISSUED THE FOLLOWING RULING: 1) THE DEFENDANTS ARE PROHIBITED FROM INITIATING ANY CONTRACT WITH FRONTIER; 2) DEFENDANTS ARE PROHIBITED FROM ANY FURTHER USE OF ANY INFORMATION TAKEN FROM THE PLAINTIFF, REQUIRING DEFENDANTS TO RETURN OR DESTROY ALL COPIES OF ANY SUCH INFORMATION GENERATED FROM IT WITH THE SUPERVISION OF PLAINTIFF; 3) DEFENDANTS MUST ALSO PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFF A LIST OF RECIPIENTS OF E-MAILS; 4) THE DEFENDANTS MUST NOT DESTROY ANY COMMUNICATIONS, WHICH THEY HAVE A RECORD WITH ANY CLIENT OR PROSPECTIVE CLIENTS TO WHOM THOSE E-MAILS WERE SENT WITHOUT THE SUPERVISION OF THE PLAINTIFF; 5) COOPERATE IN TERMS OF COMMUNICATING TO CORRECT RECORD. BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $75,000.00 TO BE POSTED. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Sonia Wong, CSR # 11288 =301/PJB

Osc Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-01-2011 Continued To Sep-06-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
OSC Regarding Preliminary Injunction is CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO SEPTEMBER 6, 2011. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Carol A. Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
